payload.title: Tier 0 — Close the cross-crate moat
payload.description:
Complete the one feature Clippy structurally cannot replicate: named-argument (R0042) enforcement across crate boundaries.

Today R0042 stops at the crate boundary — the per-crate CalleeRegistry only sees in-crate fn declarations, and trust-std is a hand-built signature index used as a proof of concept. Until a dependency's signatures can be extracted automatically, the positional-swap bug class (the only audited bug Clippy can never catch, per README/WHY.md) still slips through on every third-party API. docs/WHY.md calls this 'the dialect's largest open gap.'

This epic closes that gap so the headline feature holds end to end.
